title: Enforcement Response
description: Response from RuleSentry enforcement API. Contains compliance decision, violations, and transformed content. v2 adds denormalized rule metadata to Violation objects for improved UI performance (category_id, profiles, regions).

is_compliant.description: Overall compliance decision: true if content passes all rules, false if any violations occurred.

decision.description: Final enforcement decision based on policy evaluation: allow (content passes), block (content rejected), transform (content modified).

violations.description: List of rule violations detected during enforcement. Empty array if no violations.

rejected_matches.description: Pattern matches that were rejected by validators. Used for debugging false negatives and tuning patterns. These are NOT violations - they represent patterns that matched but failed validation (e.g., invalid SSN area codes, failed checksums, rejected by context filters). Only populated when validators reject matches.

transformed_content.description: Content after applying transformations (masking, redaction, etc.). Only present if decision = 'transform'. Null otherwise.

transform_artifacts.description: Metadata about transformations applied. Present when decision = 'transform'.

TransformArtifacts.description: Metadata about transformations applied during enforcement. Includes statistics and reversible mappings for pseudonymization.

placeholder_stats.description: Count of placeholders assigned by category (e.g., { 'person': 3, 'organization': 2 }).

reference_dates_used.description: List of reference date anchor names used for relativization.

generalization_count.description: Number of numeric values that were generalized.

total_transforms.description: Total number of individual transformations applied.

pseudonym_mappings.description: Map of original values to their pseudonymized replacements (e.g., { 'John': 'Matthew', 'Acme Corp': 'TechVentures' }). Enables consistent pseudonymization across documents.

token_mappings.description: Map of tokens to original values for reversible tokenization (e.g., { '[TOK_5918]': 'Sarah Johnson' }). Only included when reversible=true.

Violation.description: A single rule violation detected during enforcement.

rule_id.description: Identifier of the rule that triggered this violation.

matched_value.description: The actual value that triggered the violation (e.g., matched SSN, email). May be redacted for sensitive data.

replacement.description: The value that replaced matched_value in transformed_content. For MASK: masked string (e.g., '***-**-6789'). For REPLACE: token (e.g., '[SSN_FORMAT]'). For REDACT: marker (e.g., '[REDACTED]'). For PSEUDONYMIZE: pseudonym used. Matches what appears in transformed_content.

target.description: Logical target where violation was found (e.g., 'request.body', 'response.headers.authorization').

effect.description: Effect applied by this rule (allow, block, transform, info).

start.description: Start position of the match in the input text (byte offset). Used for position-based transformation optimization. Optional for backwards compatibility.

end.description: End position of the match in the input text (byte offset). Used for position-based transformation optimization. Optional for backwards compatibility.

category_id.description: Denormalized category_id from the rule that triggered this violation (e.g., 'pii', 'phi', 'financial'). Enables UI to filter/group violations by category without looking up rule metadata. v2 addition for performance.

profiles.description: Denormalized profiles array from the rule that triggered this violation (e.g., ['hipaa', 'production']). Enables UI to filter violations by profile without looking up rule metadata. v2 addition for performance.

regions.description: Denormalized regions array from the rule that triggered this violation. Supports hierarchical regions (e.g., ['US', 'US-CA'] or ['EU', 'DE-BY']). Enables UI to display region context without looking up rule metadata. v2 addition for performance, renamed from 'jurisdictions' in v4.

validator_name.description: Name of the validator used to verify this match (e.g., 'luhn', 'ssn_format', 'nhs_mod11'). Null if no validator was configured (pattern-only match). Used for debugging and understanding why a rule triggered.

validator_status.description: Status of validator execution: 'passed' (validator accepted the match), 'failed' (validator rejected the match - should not appear in violations), 'not_applicable' (no validator configured). Used for debugging rule matching behavior.

validator_details.description: Detailed information about validator execution. Present when validator_name is set. Enables debugging false positives/negatives by showing intermediate computation steps.

ValidatorDetails.description: Detailed information about validator execution for debugging purposes.

algorithm.description: Human-readable description of the validation algorithm (e.g., 'Luhn checksum (ISO/IEC 7812-1)', 'SSA Publication No. 05-10633 format validation').

cleaned_value.description: The matched value after applying strip_chars normalization (e.g., '4532123456789010' from '4532-1234-5678-9010'). Used by checksum algorithms.

checksum_computed.description: The computed checksum or validation result (e.g., '0' for Luhn mod 10, area/group/serial for SSN). Helps debug checksum failures.

reject_reason.description: Reason why validator rejected the match (e.g., 'Test keyword "example card" found', 'Invalid area code 666'). Present when validation fails due to reject_keywords or format constraints.

RejectedMatch.description: A pattern match that was rejected by a validator. Used for debugging and pattern tuning.

rule_id.description: Identifier of the rule whose pattern matched.

matched_value.description: The value that matched the pattern but was rejected by the validator.

start.description: Start position of the match in the input text (byte offset).

end.description: End position of the match in the input text (byte offset).

validator_name.description: Name of the validator that rejected this match (e.g., 'ssn_format', 'luhn', 'nhs_mod11').

validator_status.description: Status of validator execution. Will always be 'failed' for rejected matches.

validator_details.description: Detailed information about why the validator rejected this match. Includes reject_reason explaining the failure.

violations.message: Social Security Number detected in request body
